definitions.text: The act of seizing or capturing.
examples: The catch of the perpetrator was the product of a year of police work.

definitions.text: The act of catching an object in motion, especially a ball.
examples: The player made an impressive catch.

definitions.text: The act of noticing, understanding or hearing.
examples: Good catch. I never would have remembered that.

definitions.text: To capture, overtake. To capture or snare (someone or something which would rather escape). .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: I hope I catch a fish.
He ran but we caught him at the exit.

definitions.text: To capture or snare (someone or something which would rather escape). .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: I hope I catch a fish.
He ran but we caught him at the exit.

definitions.text: To entrap or trip up a person; to deceive.
